transcript: There's the lineup, contesting the gold, silver, bronze, and lesser placings, the top eight in the world at the moment, the 200 individual medley. Lofty has 11 Olympic medals, which is fairly impressive. This man, 25, 21 gold medals. Pereira, Dan Wallace. Gosh, he's up and down here to pour Olympic Games trials after a great world championship. Well, they're chanting Pereira's name, his Christian name. It's been to six world championships, Pereira, four Olympic Games, he's hung on for a home Olympic Games. That's good. They allowed the crowd to be entertained and enjoy themselves, voice their support. Now they'll just get the hush from this bunch of Germany. Now they'll just get the hush. So here's Heinz of Germany. Von Kuhn is in seven, Argenau in six, Lochte in five. This man is in four, he's Lochte. So when you look at them, it'll be Phelps in lane four, and either side of them, Pereira and Lochte. 200 individual medals, so each of the disciplines Feltz alongside of him And Lochte too And Hageno, they're all in a line. Pereira just edging slightly in front at this early stage into the butterfly. They're going to go into the backstroke now. Does he take the honors? The crowd will go crazy. They do. 24-7-4. Phelps is lurking though. 0.17 behind. Phelps' underwaters are so good in this tournament. I am don't't write off Ryan Lochte. He's a past champion, a 200 backstroke. He's going to try and go with Phelps here. And Hageno as well. They've all got good breaststroke form, but there's three out there now, Pereira, Phelps and Lochte. Phelps may be in front, just world record pace. This is sensational. Now, how does Pereira go in the breaststroke? He was found a little bit wanting in the semis, but he's still there now. Phelps' underwater was pretty impressive in the breaststroke. His breaststroke has been working pretty well for him at this Olympic campaign in 2016. But he's got some company. He's not shaking them off as easy as some of the other events. But once he hits the freestyle, his freestyle has really been firing well here in Rio. And amazingly, he is right on the world record line. Fujimori in lane one, moves up to fourth. But it's Phelps, Pereira, Lochte, Brazil looking for a middle. The crowd deafening here. The mighty Michael Phelps. The champion is leading. He's had a very big campaign. Pereira is throwing everything at him. But Phelps is increasing the lead, increasing the stroke rate. There has never been a better swimmer. The most decorated swimmer is miles ahead. Michael Phelps, extraordinary yet again, 1.95 seconds was the margin in victory, four times he wins the 200 metres individual medley, the swimmer male or female to win the same event on four occasions absolutely extraordinary not enough that he sets the history with the amount of medals but now four successful gold medals consecutively in the 200 im just incredible well for a long time in that race I thought we were going to get three swimmers in their 30s taking all the medals because it was Phelps and Lottie and Pereira for most of that race. But then Hageno came through and also Wong and they take the silver and bronze medals behind Michael Phelps. We'll be right back. came through and also Wong and they take the silver and bronze medals behind Michael Phelps I think he's gonna be emotional emotional here. Michael Phelps. What superlatives will be used around the world over the next few hours for this man? No-one has achieved. No-one's got close to achieving what he has. Remember, he thought three was enough. Now I'm finished. The olympian certainly he came back this is emotional Obrigado por assistir
